paper_title: Experimental and Numerical Observation of Dark and Bright Breathers in the Band Gap of a Diatomic Electrical Lattice

methods.description: Setup of an electrical lattice using RLC resonators to create a diatomic system for experimental observation of breathers.

steps.description: Incorporate two different cells, (a) and (b), with different values of inductors, L2, located on alternate sites to make the lattice a diatomic system.
steps.searchable_summary: configure diatomic electrical lattice with alternating inductor values
inputs: inductor values L2 for cells a and b

steps.description: Connect neighboring nodes inductively via inductors, L1, and drive them with a sinusoidal signal via a resistor, R.
steps.searchable_summary: connect nodes with inductors and drive with sinusoidal signal

steps.description: Implement periodic boundary conditions by connecting the last circuit cell to the first.
steps.searchable_summary: apply periodic boundary conditions to electrical lattice

steps.description: Institute a phase shift of π between two consecutive nodes of the same type to excite zone boundary plane-wave modes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply phase shift to excite plane-wave modes

methods.description: Measurement of node voltages in the electrical lattice using a multichannel analog-to-digital converter.

steps.description: Measure node voltages Vn at points A at a rate of 2.5 MHz using a multichannel analog-to-digital converter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure node voltages with ADC at 2.5 MHz

methods.description: Setup of a theoretical model to describe the dynamics of the electrical network using dimensionless equations of motion.

steps.description: Introduce an amplitude-dependent phenomenological resistor, Rl, in parallel to the cell capacitance to reproduce experimentally observed features.
steps.searchable_summary: model electrical network with amplitude-dependent resistor

steps.description: Use dimensionless variables to describe the dynamics of the electrical network.
steps.searchable_summary: apply dimensionless variables to model dynamics
